{"key_event":"Estallido social and constitutional churn","reasoning":"Mass mobilization, feminist and Indigenous organizing, pension withdrawals, plebiscites, and two constitutional rejections show civil society gained exceptional agenda-setting and veto power, though fragmentation limits coherent control. The state retained strong administrative and coercive capacity through COVID measures, vaccination, emergency rule, and a tougher security agenda, but institutional distrust, crime pressures, fiscal constraints, and contested authority reduced its effective legitimacy and reach from the prior decade.","society_change":0.5,"society_power":8.6,"state_change":-0.5,"state_power":8.4}
